backstory: Mid-level engineer at a 50-person Series B company. Working in a turbo-managed monorepo. Sentry already deployed company-wide. Wants tools that help *them* personally, not the org.

repo_signals.stack: turbo monorepo, next.js + nest.js + postgres + redis

expected_wow: Two-stage. (1) Four-pillar sweep at turn 2: 'Who's working on it with you' shows the user is one of many on a 50-person team (git log shows lots of commit authors); 'What we noticed' surfaces personal-scope items (your stale PRs, your old issues, your last 3 PRs took avg 4 days to land). The 'What we can connect to' pillar is honest: gh + Sentry SDK + dependabot + CODEOWNERS (no Stripe — internal product). (2) Recommend opens post-connect at turn 4 with a `light` move scoped to PERSONAL surface ('Your last 3 PRs averaged 4d to land — my read is @mur/reviewer would unblock you here. Want me to install, or describe what'd actually help?'). The persona-level move is to keep recommendations personal-scope, not org-scope.
expected_dropoff_risk: Org policy on third-party connects (turn 3). GitHub-App per-repo scoping should help. The four-pillar sweep's 'What we can connect to' line reassures that Mur sees their company-mature setup correctly — no overreach, no padding.
wow_score_target: ≥ 2 in H5 (signal specificity), H8 (chief-of-staff voice), H9 (recommend propose breadth — narrow but honest when probed), H10 (every propose grounded), H11 (four pillars including the team-density observation in 'Who'), H12 + H13 + H15 yes
scoring_notes: Infra-mature persona. H9 score 2 acceptable: even when probed, recommend's propose surfaces narrow set (Wire @mur/reviewer, daily digest, maybe Linear-connect). Adapts honestly — doesn't pad with security-audit when no risky_patterns exist. Watch H11: 'Who's working on it with you' should NOT collapse to 'just you' — git log shows real team density. Watch H15: recommend's opening is single grounded propose, not a menu of org-tooling.
